ranked_movies.title: Melancholia
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as a quintessential film about depression and hopelessness, with a powerful portrayal of the protagonist's mental state.

ranked_movies.title: The Master
ranked_movies.reason: Joaquin Phoenix's performance as Freddie Quell is highlighted as one of the best portrayals of suicidal depression in cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Manchester by the Sea
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times for its raw and realistic depiction of grief, depression, and emotional turmoil.

ranked_movies.title: Requiem for a Dream
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently cited as one of the most depressing films from beginning to end, focusing on addiction and despair.

ranked_movies.title: Synecdoche, New York
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its exploration of existential dread, depression, and the complexities of life and art.

ranked_movies.title: Her
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed for its themes of loneliness, connection, and the emotional struggles of the protagonist in a modern world.

ranked_movies.title: Lars and the Real Girl
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its sensitive portrayal of mental health, loneliness, and the community's support for a struggling individual.

ranked_movies.title: The Whale
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for its heartbreaking depiction of isolation, guilt, and depression, with a focus on the protagonist's physical and emotional struggles.

ranked_movies.title: Belladonna of Sadness
ranked_movies.reason: Described as a visually striking and deeply depressing animated film that explores trauma and despair.

ranked_movies.title: Stay
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its psychological depth and portrayal of mental illness, confusion, and reality distortion.

ranked_movies.title: Drive
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed for its protagonist's emotional isolation, stoicism, and underlying depression amidst a violent world.

ranked_movies.title: Little Miss Sunshine
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for its blend of humor and sadness, focusing on a family's struggles with depression, failure, and hope.

ranked_movies.title: Three Colors Blue
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted for its profound exploration of grief, loss, and the gradual process of healing and emotional rediscovery.

ranked_movies.title: Wendy and Lucy
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its minimalist and realistic portrayal of economic hardship, loneliness, and despair.

ranked_movies.title: On the Count of Three
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its dark humor and honest depiction of suicide, friendship, and mental health struggles.

ranked_movies.title: Perfect Blue
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed for its psychological horror, identity crisis, and the depressing descent into madness of its protagonist.

ranked_movies.title: Naked
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for its gritty and bleak exploration of nihilism, despair, and societal decay through the eyes of its protagonist.

ranked_movies.title: Incendies
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a profoundly depressing film dealing with war, family secrets, and traumatic revelations.

ranked_movies.title: What Dreams May Come
ranked_movies.reason: Noted for its visual beauty and heartbreaking story about love, loss, and the afterlife, often described as emotionally draining.

ranked_movies.title: Schindler's List
ranked_movies.reason: Included for its historical gravity and overwhelming depiction of human suffering, though not solely focused on individual depression.
